recipes.name: Kodachrome 25
recipes.film: Slowest, sharpest Kodachrome — crisp slide color of 60s-70s reportage

recipes.name: Fuji Eterna
recipes.film: Fuji cinema stock — flat, calm, low-contrast look made for storytelling
recipes.tip: Great video default; leaves room to grade.

recipes.name: Astia
recipes.film: Fuji slide film tuned for skin — soft contrast, gentle faithful color

recipes.name: Acros XY
recipes.film: Acros with a yellow filter — slightly darker skies, natural skin
recipes.tip: Yellow-filter look: the classic all-round B&W choice.

recipes.name: Acros XR
recipes.film: Acros with a red filter — dramatic dark skies, glowing skin
recipes.tip: Red-filter look: strongest sky separation, great with clouds.

recipes.name: Acros XG
recipes.film: Acros with a green filter — brighter foliage, textured skin tones
recipes.tip: Green-filter look: best for greenery and male portraits.

recipes.name: Phenomena
recipes.film: Author original — saturated, warm, slightly surreal color fantasy

recipes.name: Crimson
recipes.film: Author original — red-leaning warmth with rich magenta cast

recipes.name: Arista Edu 100
recipes.film: Budget student B&W film — honest mid-contrast, classic grain feel

recipes.name: Acidnom
recipes.film: Author original — acid-bright palette with twisted color shifts

recipes.name: GAF 500
recipes.film: Discontinued 70s high-speed film — coarse, warm, unmistakably retro

recipes.name: Senova Light
recipes.film: Author original — airy pastel light with a gentle glow
recipes.tip: Has separate day (8000K B2-M2) and night (2900K A5-G1) white balance.

recipes.name: Zero Mute
recipes.film: Author original — restrained, desaturated calm with soft tones

recipes.name: Neo Max
recipes.film: Author original B&W — modern deep blacks with smooth midtones

recipes.name: Kosmo Pan
recipes.film: Kosmo Foto Mono 100 — Ukrainian-rolled classic pan B&W, soft and filmic

recipes.name: MidRed Infra
recipes.film: Infrared-inspired B&W — bright foliage, black skies, surreal contrast

recipes.name: Ilford HP5
recipes.film: The workhorse of British B&W — forgiving, gritty, endlessly classic

recipes.name: Vespera - Nightime
recipes.film: Author original for night — tungsten glow kept warm and cinematic
recipes.tip: Built for artificial light; try it at dusk too.

recipes.name: Veniliqum - Night
recipes.film: Author original for night — bold color under neon and streetlights

recipes.name: Gold Luxe - Nighttime
recipes.film: Author original — gilded night look; also lovely in daylight
recipes.tip: Author: nighttime recipes like Gold Luxe look fantastic during the day too.

recipes.name: Estra 500
recipes.film: Author original — subtle dreamy haze with muted pastel color

recipes.name: Rose Spectra
recipes.film: Author original — rosy warmth flattering for skin and travel light

recipes.name: Zetra 100
recipes.film: Author original — balanced neutral negative with quiet character

recipes.name: Oktar
recipes.film: Author original — dense, saturated warmth made for nature

recipes.name: DreamNeg
recipes.film: Author original — hazy dreamlike negative with lifted shadows

recipes.name: Classic Cinema
recipes.film: Vintage Hollywood print feel — teal-leaning shadows, warm skin
recipes.tip: AWB White Priority makes it easy under changing light.

recipes.name: CineChrome
recipes.film: Author original — chrome-slide color with a cinematic curve
recipes.tip: HLG3 gamma: turn Gamma Assist off.

recipes.name: Pro Color
recipes.film: Author original — punchy commercial color, crisp and confident
recipes.tip: HLG3 gamma: turn Gamma Assist off.

recipes.name: RedScale Ultra
recipes.film: Film loaded backwards — everything bathed in orange-red fire
recipes.tip: WB needs a custom reading off a teal card (>9900K).

recipes.name: Amarelo 30D
recipes.film: Author original — yellow-gold daylight negative, sunny and dense

recipes.name: MagicSpice
recipes.film: Author original — golden all-rounder with a spicy warm kick
recipes.tip: One of the most versatile recipes in the pack.

recipes.name: Chroma Fade
recipes.film: Faded-memory B&W with a whisper of color response left in

recipes.name: T-Max 100
recipes.film: Kodak's modern fine-grain B&W — clinical sharpness, smooth tones

recipes.name: Delta 3200
recipes.film: Ilford's high-speed B&W — big grain, deep mood, made for dim light

recipes.name: Cinestill X
recipes.film: Eastman Double-X — the timeless cinema B&W of noir and Oppenheimer

recipes.name: X-Tarr
recipes.film: Author original — the deepest blacks in the pack, near-void contrast

recipes.name: Agfa Precisa
recipes.film: Agfa slide film loved for cross-processing — cool teal-blue twist

recipes.name: Phenix Harman
recipes.film: Harman Phoenix 200 — experimental new film, bold halation-like warmth

recipes.name: Ayon 200
recipes.film: Author original — deep yellow cast, like memories left in the sun

recipes.name: Asteroid City - Kodak Vision T200
recipes.film: Wes Anderson's pastel desert — Kodak Vision3 200T pushed to pastel
recipes.tip: S-Log gamma: turn Gamma Assist off.

recipes.name: Sony Eterna
recipes.film: Eterna mood rebuilt for Sony — soft cinematic base for stills or video

recipes.name: EktaChrome
recipes.film: Kodak's revived slide film — cool-clean color, editorial feel

recipes.name: Leicachrome
recipes.film: Leica-flavored chrome — restrained color with expensive contrast

recipes.name: Blue Velvet / Cinestill 50D
recipes.film: Cinestill 50D by day — fine grain, gentle blue-leaning daylight color

recipes.name: Kodak Tri-X 1600 Pushed
recipes.film: Tri-X pushed two stops — punchy contrast and heavy honest grain
recipes.tip: Shoot ISO 1600+ and turn JPG noise reduction off for real grain.

recipes.name: Kodak Tri-X 400
recipes.film: The most famous B&W film ever — soft flat contrast, mid grain
recipes.tip: Shoot ISO 1600+ with noise reduction set to Low for the grain.

recipes.name: Fortia 50
recipes.film: Fuji's rarest slide film — maximum saturation, made for flowers

recipes.name: Provia RX
recipes.film: Author's richer Provia — slide color with extra bite
